steps.title: Pin down what the underlined sentence actually says
steps.content:
The underlined sentence reads: “The new six-point scale considers varying levels of supporting evidence used to identify bioluminescent species.” It tells us what the scale uses as its basis — specifically, levels of supporting evidence. The sentences after it give examples of how species score differently based on evidence strength.
  • Choice A — 'It emphasizes the thoroughness of the research conducted.': The sentence says nothing about how thorough the researchers were — it describes the scale’s criteria, not the effort behind it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It explains how the new classification system was derived from the previous binary system.': No comparison to the binary system is made in the underlined sentence; that background appears only in the first sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It justifies the need for a new classification system for marine species.': The justification (limitations of binary systems) is provided before the underlined sentence, not in it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It explains the basis for the new system’s scoring criteria.': “Considers varying levels of supporting evidence” directly states what drives the six-point scores — exactly the scoring criteria the examples then illustrate. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The underlined sentence tells readers that the scale is built on “varying levels of supporting evidence,” which is the scoring basis the subsequent examples (firefly squid at 1, emperor squid at 4, shaggy anglerfish at 6) depend on. That matches choice D — D is correct.
